ingredients.item:
Originally from Northern Vietnam, bánh cuốn (which translates to “rolled cake”) are thin, steamed rice flour rolls. The rice crêpes used are similar to delicate rice noodle sheets, but smaller, thinner, and more translucent. This is a time-consuming recipe, and, unfortunately, the specialized steamer necessary to make bánh cuốn is hard to find outside of Vietnam; however, you can use an 8-inch (20 cm) lightweight nonstick pan instead.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD5 or 6 rolls

FILLING
¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil
1 tablespoon chopped leek (white part only)
ingredients.original:
Originally from Northern Vietnam, bánh cuốn (which translates to “rolled cake”) are thin, steamed rice flour rolls. The rice crêpes used are similar to delicate rice noodle sheets, but smaller, thinner, and more translucent. This is a time-consuming recipe, and, unfortunately, the specialized steamer necessary to make bánh cuốn is hard to find outside of Vietnam; however, you can use an 8-inch (20 cm) lightweight nonstick pan instead.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD5 or 6 rolls

FILLING
¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil
1 tablespoon chopped leek (white part only)

ingredients.item: 2 ounces (50 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and julienned (see Note)
ingredients.original: 2 ounces (50 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and julienned (see Note)

ingredients.item: 1 medium carrot, peeled and julienned (see Note)
ingredients.original: 1 medium carrot, peeled and julienned (see Note)

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) jicama, peeled and julienned (see Note)
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) jicama, peeled and julienned (see Note)

ingredients.item: 2 ounces (56 g) fried tofu, cut into strips
ingredients.original: 2 ounces (56 g) fried tofu, cut into strips

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon soy sauce or vegan fish sauce
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon soy sauce or vegan fish sauce

ingredients.item:
CRÊPES
7 ounces (200 g) rice flour
7 ounces (200 g) tapioca starch
ingredients.original:
CRÊPES
7 ounces (200 g) rice flour
7 ounces (200 g) tapioca starch

ingredients.item: 4 cups (1 L) water
ingredients.original: 4 cups (1 L) water

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, plus more for greasing pan
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, plus more for greasing pan

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon salt
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon salt

ingredients.item:
FOR SERVING
1 medium cucumber, julienned (see Note)
7 ounces (200 g) mung bean sprouts, blanched
ingredients.original:
FOR SERVING
1 medium cucumber, julienned (see Note)
7 ounces (200 g) mung bean sprouts, blanched

ingredients.item: 1 cup (240 ml) vegan fish dipping sauce
ingredients.original: 1 cup (240 ml) vegan fish dipping sauce

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE FILLING Heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of vegetable oil in a medium skillet over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until golden brown (1 to 2 minutes). Transfer to a small bowl along with most of the oil (leave about 1 tablespoon of leek and oil in the skillet).
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE FILLING Heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of vegetable oil in a medium skillet over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until golden brown (1 to 2 minutes). Transfer to a small bowl along with most of the oil (leave about 1 tablespoon of leek and oil in the skillet).

ingredients.item: 2. Return pan to heat and add mushrooms, carrot, jicama, fried tofu, salt, stock powder, and soy sauce. Cook and stir over high heat until vegetables have softened but still retain some crispness (about 3 minutes), and then transfer to a medium bowl to cool.
ingredients.original: 2. Return pan to heat and add mushrooms, carrot, jicama, fried tofu, salt, stock powder, and soy sauce. Cook and stir over high heat until vegetables have softened but still retain some crispness (about 3 minutes), and then transfer to a medium bowl to cool.

ingredients.item: 3. MAKE THE CRÊPES In a large bowl, combine rice flour, tapioca starch, water, 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, and salt, and stir well until flour is dissolved. Let the batter rest for at least 1 hour or overnight.
ingredients.original: 3. MAKE THE CRÊPES In a large bowl, combine rice flour, tapioca starch, water, 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, and salt, and stir well until flour is dissolved. Let the batter rest for at least 1 hour or overnight.

ingredients.item: 4. Grease a large plate, tray, or cutting board with some of the remaining leek oil and grease an 8-inch (20 cm) nonstick pan with a very thin layer of vegetable oil.
ingredients.original: 4. Grease a large plate, tray, or cutting board with some of the remaining leek oil and grease an 8-inch (20 cm) nonstick pan with a very thin layer of vegetable oil.

ingredients.item: 5. Heat the nonstick pan over medium heat. When it's hot, stir the batter and pour about ¼ cup (60 ml) of it into the pan. While wearing oven mitts, tilt the pan in a circular motion to thoroughly coat the bottom with batter and create an evenly round crêpe. Cover and allow the crêpe to steam until it turns translucent (about 1 minute). Uncover, lift the pan, and flip the crêpe out onto your pre-greased plate, tray, or cutting board. (Use a spatula to loosen before flipping if some parts are sticking to the pan). Repeat with remaining batter.
ingredients.original: 5. Heat the nonstick pan over medium heat. When it's hot, stir the batter and pour about ¼ cup (60 ml) of it into the pan. While wearing oven mitts, tilt the pan in a circular motion to thoroughly coat the bottom with batter and create an evenly round crêpe. Cover and allow the crêpe to steam until it turns translucent (about 1 minute). Uncover, lift the pan, and flip the crêpe out onto your pre-greased plate, tray, or cutting board. (Use a spatula to loosen before flipping if some parts are sticking to the pan). Repeat with remaining batter.

ingredients.item: 6. While waiting for the next crêpe to steam, top the finished one with 2 tablespoons of the filling. Spread it across the crêpe until it is closer to the bottom end, and then lift up the bottom end and roll it over the filling. Secure the roll closed, gently but tightly.
ingredients.original: 6. While waiting for the next crêpe to steam, top the finished one with 2 tablespoons of the filling. Spread it across the crêpe until it is closer to the bottom end, and then lift up the bottom end and roll it over the filling. Secure the roll closed, gently but tightly.

ingredients.item: 7. FOR SERVING Arrange the rolls on a platter and spoon the reserved fried leek and oil on top. Serve with julienned cucumbers, mung bean sprouts, and vegan fish dipping sauce.
ingredients.original: 7. FOR SERVING Arrange the rolls on a platter and spoon the reserved fried leek and oil on top. Serve with julienned cucumbers, mung bean sprouts, and vegan fish dipping sauce.
